clusters.title: Facing bipartisan criticism of Iran deal, Trump lashes out at "fools"
bullet_summary: Reported by CBS News, Financial Times, Al Jazeera and others

items.title: Facing bipartisan criticism of Iran deal, Trump lashes out at "fools"
items.standfirst: Republican Senators Bill Cassidy, Thom Tillis, Ted Cruz and Tom Cotton have been critical of the 14-point memorandum of understanding signed on Wednesday.
items.content: Republican Senators Bill Cassidy, Thom Tillis, Ted Cruz and Tom Cotton have been critical of the 14-point memorandum of understanding signed on Wednesday.

items.title: Democrats and some Republicans fuming at Trump's Iran deal
items.standfirst: Vice President JD Vance defended President Trump's deal with Iran, which he signed on Wednesday at the Palace of Versailles in France. Weijia Jiang has more reaction and details.
items.content: Vice President JD Vance defended President Trump's deal with Iran, which he signed on Wednesday at the Palace of Versailles in France. Weijia Jiang has more reaction and details.

items.title: Details after Trump signs peace deal with Iran
items.standfirst: The clock started ticking for the U.S. and Iran to reach a final long-term peace deal after the presidents of both countries signed off on the 14-point memorandum of understanding on Wednesday. CBS News White House reporter Aaron Navarro has more.
items.content: The clock started ticking for the U.S. and Iran to reach a final long-term peace deal after the presidents of both countries signed off on the 14-point memorandum of understanding on Wednesday. CBS News White House reporter Aaron Navarro has more.

items.title: H. R. McMaster says preliminary Iran deal "cuts very significantly against U.S. interests"
items.standfirst: President Trump's memorandum of understanding with Iran is being met with criticism from both Republicans and Democrats. H. R. McMaster, former national security adviser to Mr. Trump, joins with his perspective on the deal.
items.content: President Trump's memorandum of understanding with Iran is being met with criticism from both Republicans and Democrats. H. R. McMaster, former national security adviser to Mr. Trump, joins with his perspective on the deal.

items.title: Initial U.S.-Iran deal draws mixed reactions from Republicans
items.standfirst: President Trump signed a memorandum of understanding with Iran on Wednesday night. The reviews are in, and Senate Republicans were either critical or cautiously hopeful that something better could be achieved. CBS News' Weijia Jiang and Nikole Killion have the latest.
items.content: President Trump signed a memorandum of understanding with Iran on Wednesday night. The reviews are in, and Senate Republicans were either critical or cautiously hopeful that something better could be achieved. CBS News' Weijia Jiang and Nikole Killion have the latest.

items.content: JD Vance slammed members of Israel’s government for criticising US President Donald Trump over his Iran deal efforts.

clusters.title: Ukraine launches drone attack on Moscow: "If Ukraine burns, then your Moscow will burn as well"
clusters.neutral_headline: Ukraine hits a Moscow oil refinery and other sites in a large-scale drone attack
bullet_summary: Ukraine's President Volodymyr Zelenskyy said it was a retaliatory act
Reported by CBS News, NPR News, BBC World and others

items.title: Ukraine launches drone attack on Moscow: "If Ukraine burns, then your Moscow will burn as well"
items.standfirst: Ukraine hammered Russia with the largest attack on the capital city of Moscow since Russia invaded its neighbor more than four years ago. A massive swarm of Ukrainian drones hit a major oil refinery there, among other targets, on Thursday. Ukraine's President Volodymyr Zelenskyy said it was a retaliatory act. Aidan Stretch reports.
items.content: Ukraine hammered Russia with the largest attack on the capital city of Moscow since Russia invaded its neighbor more than four years ago. A massive swarm of Ukrainian drones hit a major oil refinery there, among other targets, on Thursday. Ukraine's President Volodymyr Zelenskyy said it was a retaliatory act. Aidan Stretch reports.

items.title: Ukraine hits a Moscow oil refinery and other sites in a large-scale drone attack
items.standfirst: Ukraine launched a wave of drone attacks on Russia early Thursday — one of the largest attacks on Russia's capital since the Kremlin ordered the invasion of Ukraine more than four years ago.
items.content: Ukraine launched a wave of drone attacks on Russia early Thursday — one of the largest attacks on Russia's capital since the Kremlin ordered the invasion of Ukraine more than four years ago.(Image credit: AFP)

items.title: Moscow residents complain of black rain after largest Ukrainian attack hits oil refinery
items.standfirst: A refinery and a shopping centre burned after almost 200 Ukrainian drones struck an area to the south-east of the Russian capital.
items.content: A refinery and a shopping centre burned after almost 200 Ukrainian drones struck an area to the south-east of the Russian capital.

items.title: Ukraine unleashes biggest attack on Moscow in years, setting a key oil refinery ablaze
items.standfirst: Waves of Ukrainian drones hit a major oil refinery for the second time this week, disrupting flights and sending thick clouds of black smoke rising over the city Thursday morning.
items.content: Waves of Ukrainian drones hit a major oil refinery for the second time this week, disrupting flights and sending thick clouds of black smoke rising over the city Thursday morning.

items.title: Ukraine hits Moscow with largest-ever drone attack
items.standfirst: Almost 200 drones strike Russian capital, several damaging the city’s largest oil refinery
items.content: Almost 200 drones strike Russian capital, several damaging the city’s largest oil refinery

items.content: The New York Knicks celebrated their first NBA title in 53 years with a frenzied ticker-tape parade through Manhattan.

clusters.title: Obamas host star-studded opening of Chicago presidential centre
clusters.neutral_headline: Obamas host star-studded opening of Chicago presidential centre
bullet_summary: “I hope this center will serve as an affirmation of how precious our democracy truly is,” Obama said, as Stevie Wonder closed the celebration
Reported by BBC World, CBS News, France24

items.title: Obamas host star-studded opening of Chicago presidential centre
items.standfirst: The campus opens to the public this weekend with a museum, community amenities and more to honour the 44th US president.
items.content: The campus opens to the public this weekend with a museum, community amenities and more to honour the 44th US president.

items.title: Obama Presidential Center opens: "We wanted it to be a vibrant, living celebration of community"
items.standfirst: Former President Barack Obama formally unveiled his presidential center in Chicago on Thursday, calling it an "expression of thanks" to the city and a monument, not to his story, but to the U.S.'s story. Caitlin Huey-Burns reports.
items.content: Former President Barack Obama formally unveiled his presidential center in Chicago on Thursday, calling it an "expression of thanks" to the city and a monument, not to his story, but to the U.S.'s story. Caitlin Huey-Burns reports.

items.title: Obama Presidential Center opens in Chicago with star-studded celebrations
items.standfirst: The Obama Presidential Center's grand opening ceremony featured a lineup of tributes from his family and closest colleagues, and musical performances by Bruce Springsteen, The Roots and more.
items.content: The Obama Presidential Center's grand opening ceremony featured a lineup of tributes from his family and closest colleagues, and musical performances by Bruce Springsteen, The Roots and more.

items.title: How the Obama Presidential Center is unique among presidential libraries
items.standfirst: Former presidents and world leaders gathered in Chicago on Thursday to celebrate the grand opening of the Obama Presidential Center. CBS News congressional correspondent Caitlin Huey-Burns reports.
items.content: Former presidents and world leaders gathered in Chicago on Thursday to celebrate the grand opening of the Obama Presidential Center. CBS News congressional correspondent Caitlin Huey-Burns reports.

items.title: Obama opens Chicago presidential center, urges Americans to defend democracy
items.standfirst: Former US President Barack Obama opened his presidential center in Chicago on Thursday, calling for the defense of democracy in a star-studded event attended by three former presidents. “I hope this center will serve as an affirmation of how precious our democracy truly is,” Obama said, as Stevie Wonder closed the celebration.
items.content: Former US President Barack Obama opened his presidential center in Chicago on Thursday, calling for the defense of democracy in a star-studded event attended by three former presidents. “I hope this center will serve as an affirmation of how precious our democracy truly is,” Obama said, as Stevie Wonder closed the celebration.

items.title: 6/18: The Takeout with Major Garrett

items.content: Vice President JD Vance warns Israel to abide by initial U.S.-Iran deal; Obama Presidential Center holds star-studded grand opening ceremony.

items.title: Obama Presidential Center grand opening ceremony wraps in Chicago
items.standfirst: The Obama Presidential Center held a star-studded grand opening in Chicago on Thursday. CBS News Chicago's Irika Sargent joins with highlights of the ceremony.
items.content: The Obama Presidential Center held a star-studded grand opening in Chicago on Thursday. CBS News Chicago's Irika Sargent joins with highlights of the ceremony.

A three-year-old boy was taken to Addenbrooke's Hospital with critical injuries and is in a stable condition, Cambridgeshire Police said

items.title: 'Toddler critical' and 'cost of living hope'
items.standfirst: The papers focus on a three-year-old boy, who was who was left with critical injuries after ending up a crocodile enclosure in a Cambridgeshire zoo.
items.content: The papers focus on a three-year-old boy, who was who was left with critical injuries after ending up a crocodile enclosure in a Cambridgeshire zoo.

items.title: Man arrested after boy, three, injured in Cambridgeshire zoo crocodile enclosure
items.standfirst: Officers arrest man on suspicion of attempted murder as child is treated in hospital for serious injuries A man has been arrested on suspicion of attempted murder after a three-year-old boy ended up in a crocodile enclosure, Cambridgeshire police said.The force said officers were called to Johnsons of Old Hurst zoo in Huntingdonshire at 1.24pm on Thursday over “reports of an incident involving a three-year-old boy, during which he ended up in the crocodile enclosure”. Continue reading...

clusters.title: Luigi Mangione's legal team drops psychiatric defense in state murder trial
clusters.neutral_headline: Mangione lawyers abandon psychiatric defense over health CEO’s killing
bullet_summary: Reported by CBS News, Guardian World

items.title: Luigi Mangione's legal team drops psychiatric defense in state murder trial
items.standfirst: Attorneys for Luigi Mangione, the man accused of killing UnitedHealthcare CEO Brian Thompson, dropped plans for a psychiatric defense in his state case on Thursday. CBS News legal reporter Katrina Kaufman has the details.
items.content: Attorneys for Luigi Mangione, the man accused of killing UnitedHealthcare CEO Brian Thompson, dropped plans for a psychiatric defense in his state case on Thursday. CBS News legal reporter Katrina Kaufman has the details.

items.title: Mangione lawyers abandon psychiatric defense over health CEO’s killing
items.standfirst: Legal team of alleged gunman, 28, reverses position on ‘extreme emotional disturbance’ defense after one dayIn a stunning reversal, Luigi Mangione’s legal team said on Thursday they would no longer pursue a psychiatric defense in his upcoming state trial over the killing of the UnitedHealthcare CEO Brian Thompson.Just one day earlier, Mangione’s lawyers told Judge Gregory Carro they would pursue a defense claiming the 28-year-old was suffering an “extreme emotional disturbance” at the time of Thompson’s killing on 4 December 2024. Continue reading...
items.content: Legal team of alleged gunman, 28, reverses position on ‘extreme emotional disturbance’ defense after one dayIn a stunning reversal, Luigi Mangione’s legal team said on Thursday they would no longer pursue a psychiatric defense in his upcoming state trial over the killing of the UnitedHealthcare CEO Brian Thompson.Just one day earlier, Mangione’s lawyers told Judge Gregory Carro they would pursue a defense claiming the 28-year-old was suffering an “extreme emotional disturbance” at the time of Thompson’s killing on 4 December 2024. Continue reading...

items.title: Luigi Mangione's legal team withdraws psychiatric defense
items.standfirst: Luigi Mangione's legal team says they withdrew a psychiatric defense​​ in his New York state murder trial one day after telling the court they would use it.
items.content: Luigi Mangione's legal team says they withdrew a psychiatric defense​​ in his New York state murder trial one day after telling the court they would use it.

clusters.title: Nearly 160 sick with flu at US air force base after Hegseth ends mandatory vaccines
clusters.neutral_headline: Air Force unit faces flu outbreak weeks after vaccine mandate dropped
bullet_summary: Reported by Guardian World, CBS News

items.title: Nearly 160 sick with flu at US air force base after Hegseth ends mandatory vaccines
items.standfirst: Reports say Texas recruits ill from outbreak, which comes weeks after defense secretary made flu vaccine optionalAn influenza outbreak has reportedly sickened more than 150 recruits in training at Lackland air force base in San Antonio, Texas.The outbreak comes just weeks after the defense secretary, Pete Hegseth, ended mandatory flu vaccination for the military, citing the need for bodily autonomy for servicemembers. Continue reading...
items.content: Reports say Texas recruits ill from outbreak, which comes weeks after defense secretary made flu vaccine optionalAn influenza outbreak has reportedly sickened more than 150 recruits in training at Lackland air force base in San Antonio, Texas.The outbreak comes just weeks after the defense secretary, Pete Hegseth, ended mandatory flu vaccination for the military, citing the need for bodily autonomy for servicemembers. Continue reading...

items.title: Air Force unit faces flu outbreak weeks after vaccine mandate dropped
items.standfirst: Dozens of service members at Lackland Air Force Base in Texas have fallen ill with the flu in the weeks since Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth rescinded the vaccine mandate, sources familiar confirmed to CBS News.
items.content: Dozens of service members at Lackland Air Force Base in Texas have fallen ill with the flu in the weeks since Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth rescinded the vaccine mandate, sources familiar confirmed to CBS News.

clusters.title: World Cup 2026: South Africa grab draw with Czechia with late penalty
clusters.neutral_headline: World Cup 2026: South Africa grab draw with Czechia with late penalty

items.title: World Cup 2026: South Africa grab draw with Czechia with late penalty
items.standfirst: Teboho Mokoena equalises through a late penalty to salvage a draw for South Africa in their Group A clash with Czechia.
items.content: Teboho Mokoena equalises through a late penalty to salvage a draw for South Africa in their Group A clash with Czechia.

items.title: World Cup 2026: South Africa fight back to earn draw against Czech Republic
items.standfirst: South Africa kept their World Cup campaign alive on Thursday as Teboho Mokoena's late penalty secured a 1-1 draw against the Czech Republic in Atlanta, denying the Czechs a crucial victory after Michal Sadilek's sixth-minute opener.
items.content: South Africa kept their World Cup campaign alive on Thursday as Teboho Mokoena's late penalty secured a 1-1 draw against the Czech Republic in Atlanta, denying the Czechs a crucial victory after Michal Sadilek's sixth-minute opener.
